2605.30076.title: UniSteer: Text-Guided Flow Matching in Activation Space for Versatile LLM Steering
2605.30076.paper_summary: Activation-based control steers large language models (LLMs) by intervening on their internal representations during inference, and has emerged as an effective paradigm for controlling behaviors such as persona and style. However, existing methods often rely on fixed steering directions or task-specific intervention modules, making them difficult to adapt to fine-grained concepts and compositional constraints. We propose UniSteer, a text-guided activation flow matching model that learns a conditional distribution over residual-stream activations from natural-language conditions. Instead of fitting a separate intervention for each target behavior, UniSteer learns a universal conditional velocity field in activation space. At inference time, UniSteer performs flow inversion by partially transporting a source activation toward a latent state and regenerating it under a target textual condition before injecting it back into the frozen LLM. The same conditional model supports activation-space classification by selecting the textual label with the lowest reconstruction energy. Experiments on three target LLMs show that UniSteer provides a unified interface across behavioral control, truthfulness steering, fine-grained concept steering, multi-constraint instruction following, and activation-space classification.
2605.30076.paper_summary_zh: 激活基礎控制透過在推理過程中介入大型語言模型（LLMs）的內部表示來引導它們，並已成為控制個性和風格等行為的有效範式。然而，現有的方法通常依賴於固定的引導方向或特定任務的介入模塊，使其難以適應細緻的概念和組合約束。我們提出了UniSteer，一種文本引導的激活流匹配模型，該模型從自然語言條件中學習殘差流激活的條件分佈。UniSteer不需要為每個目標行為擬合單獨的介入，而是在激活空間中學習一個通用的條件速度場。在推理時，UniSteer通過部分傳輸源激活到潛在狀態並在目標文本條件下重新生成它，然後再將其注入到凍結的LLM中來執行流反演。相同的條件模型支持通過選擇重建能量最低的文本標籤來進行激活空間分類。在三個目標LLM上的實驗顯示，UniSteer在行為控制、真實性引導、細緻概念引導、多約束指令遵循和激活空間分類方面提供了統一的介面。
2605.30076.author: Yingdong Shi et.al.
2605.30076.authors: Yingdong Shi, Ruiming Zhang, Changming Li, Zhiyu Yang, Kaixing Zhang, Jingyi Yu, Kan Ren
